% Auto-generated by examples/FlowConformal/paper/tables/main_table_recall.py
% Required LaTeX packages: booktabs, makecell.
\begin{table*}[!t]
\centering
\caption{UNSAT-recall on VNN-COMP'25 benchmarks. Each cell reports \emph{UNSAT-recall}\,$\uparrow$ over the breakdown $(\#\,\mathrm{correct}\,\mathrm{UNSAT}\,/\,\#\,\mathrm{effective}\,\mathrm{UNSAT})$. An instance counts as \emph{effective UNSAT} if the VNN-COMP'25 ground-truth consensus is UNSAT or if every sound tool timed out / abstained \emph{and} no tool emitted a SAT counterexample (i.e., no falsifier found one\,---\,treated as effective UNSAT here). This denominator is the same across methods, so tools that timed out on the harder instances are correctly penalized rather than silently excluded. The numerator counts instances where the tool emitted UNSAT and the effective ground truth is UNSAT (sound violations are excluded from the numerator by construction). A dagger ($\dagger N$) annotates cells where the tool committed $N$ sound violations\,---\,$N$ matches the dagger count in the companion \% Solved table. An em-dash ($-$) indicates the verifier's loader does not support the network, the benchmark was not run, or the benchmark has no effective-UNSAT instances. \textbf{Bold} percentages indicate per-column highlights. ``Ours'' is highlighted at the top; the middle block lists deterministic sound verifiers; the bottom block lists probabilistic verifiers.}
\label{tab:main_results_recall}
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.05}
\footnotesize
